issue_title: GitHub link button's error path calls showToast('Failed to copy URL', 'error') with a hardcoded English string instead of a translation key
issue_body: In scripts/settings.js, the GitHub link button's error path calls showToast('Failed to copy URL', 'error') with a hardcoded English string instead of a translation key. Every other showToast call in the file uses t('some.key'). If a non-English user hits a clipboard failure, they'll see English regardless of their language setting. Fix is small: add a settings.githubCopyFail key to all 7 languages in translations.js and swap the hardcoded string for t('settings.githubCopyFail').
patch:
@@ -198,7 +198,7 @@
             }, 1500);
         } catch (err) {
             console.error('[CV] Failed to copy GitHub URL:', err);
-            showToast('Failed to copy URL', 'error');
+            showToast(t('settings.githubCopyFail'), 'error');
         } finally {
             document.body.removeChild(tempInput);
         }
@@ -38,6 +38,7 @@
             'settings.github': 'GitHub',
             'settings.githubTitle': 'Copy GitHub link to clipboard',
             'settings.githubCopied': 'Copied!',
+            'settings.githubCopyFail': 'Failed to copy URL',
             'settings.gameSystem': 'Game System',
             'settings.gameSystemCustom': 'Custom / Other',
             'settings.uiScale': 'UI Scale',
@@ -1337,6 +1338,7 @@
